extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first quarter of the series (early times), the signal quickly reaches a clear high peak (around the top of the range) followed by a deep dip toward the bottom of the range.  
R2: In the middle part of the series, the signal spends considerable time in a medium band, fluctuating smoothly around the center of the value range without long flat segments.  
R3: Multiple narrow, sharp spikes (up and/or down) appear, but they are relatively symmetric around the mid-range rather than being mostly on one side.  
R4: The lowest values (close to the minimum of the range) are reached relatively early or midway, not concentrated only near the end.  
R5: Overall, the series does not show a dominant long monotonic fall from high to low; instead it alternates between rises and falls throughout.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high and then shows a long, mostly monotonic decrease from near the top of the range down toward the lower part within the first third of the time axis.  
R2: After this early drop, the signal remains mostly in the lower-to-mid range for a while, without quickly returning to the initial high level.  
R3: In the middle of the series there is a broad, smoother rise forming a single dominant hump rather than several separated sharp peaks.  
R4: A very pronounced, steep fall from mid/high values down close to the minimum occurs in the later middle section (around two‑thirds of the series).  
R5: After this late steep fall, the signal climbs back only to mid‑range values and then stays relatively stable there toward the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 10% of the series, there is a pronounced local maximum above 0.7.  
R2: Within the first 20% of the series, there is at least one sharp drop from above 0.6 to below 0.3.  
R3: Between 20% and 60% of the series, values fluctuate around a mid‑range level (roughly 0.3–0.6) without staying flat for long.  
R4: In the last 40% of the series, there is at least one deep valley below 0.2 followed by a recovery above 0.5.  
R5: Peaks in the last 40% are narrower and more “spiky” (steep up and steep down) than in the middle section.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 10–15% of the series, the signal starts high (around or above 0.6) and then drops steadily to a low near 0.2 or below.  
R2: After this initial drop, between 15% and 35% of the series, the signal remains relatively moderate and smoother, mostly within 0.3–0.6 without very sharp spikes.  
R3: Around the middle (roughly 35%–45%), there is a broad peak rising from about 0.4 up to around 0.7–0.8.  
R4: Immediately after this broad middle peak, there is a sustained descent into a deep minimum below 0.15–0.2.  
R5: In the last 30% of the series, the signal climbs and then stabilizes in a mid‑range plateau (around 0.4–0.6) with smaller fluctuations than earlier.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Early segment (time 0–15) contains at least one sharp upward spike followed almost immediately by a strong drop to a low value.  
R2: Mid segment (time 15–40) stays in a moderate band, fluctuating but generally avoiding both very low and very high extremes.  
R3: Around the late-middle (time 40–55) there is at least one pronounced “V” shape: a rapid fall to a low value followed by a rapid rise.  
R4: The series shows several medium-sized peaks rather than one dominant global peak.  
R5: The ending segment (time 55–70) returns to a mid-level band, without a strong monotonic rise or fall.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Very early segment (time 0–10) starts high and then drops steeply and mostly monotonically to a low value.  
R2: After this drop, the series remains relatively low for a short time, forming a broad trough.  
R3: Mid segment (time 15–40) contains a smoother, more plateau-like rise to moderate values with fewer abrupt spikes than class 0.  
R4: Late-middle (around time 40–55) has one dominant tall peak followed by a pronounced, mostly monotonic decline to a low point.  
R5: Final segment (time 55–70) rises again from this low to a stable mid-level band, without returning to very low values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 10–15% of the series, there is at least one sharp upward spike that reaches above 0.7 followed quickly (within a few points) by a strong drop toward 0.2 or below.  
R2: After this early spike-and-drop, the signal spends most of the middle part of the series (roughly 20–60% of its length) oscillating around 0.4–0.6 without long flat segments (frequent small peaks and troughs).  
R3: In the last 30–40% of the series, there is at least one pronounced deep valley reaching near 0.1–0.2 followed by a clear recovery above 0.4.  
R4: Across the whole series, no long monotonic decrease from high (≈0.7) to low (≈0.2) persists without intermediate rebounds; drops are typically broken by brief recoveries.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins with a relatively smooth, mostly monotonic decrease from a high level (around 0.6–0.7) down to about 0.2 or lower over the first 10–20% of its length, without a strong early rebound.  
R2: After this initial fall, the signal climbs and forms a broad plateau or gentle oscillation in the mid-section (roughly 20–60% of the series) around 0.4–0.6, with fewer sharp local spikes than class 0.  
R3: In the region around 60–80% of the series, there is often a single long, steep drop from around 0.6–0.7 down close to 0.0–0.1, followed by a relatively smooth recovery.  
R4: The final segment of the series (last 20–30%) shows a smoother upward recovery to around 0.4–0.6, without large high-frequency oscillations.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Early segment (time 0–15) has a sharp upward spike above 0.8 followed by a rapid drop below 0.3.  
R2: Middle segment (time 15–40) stays mostly between 0.3 and 0.6 with small, irregular oscillations.  
R3: Late segment (time 40–55) contains one or two deep dips below 0.25 followed by sharp rebounds above 0.5.  
R4: Final segment (time 55–70) stabilizes in a mid–high range, mostly between 0.4 and 0.6 without strong trends.  
R5: Overall pattern shows several sharp, narrow spikes and troughs spread across the series, not concentrated in a single long downward slope.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Very beginning (time 0–10) starts high (around or above 0.6) and then drops steeply to around 0.2 or lower.  
R2: Early–middle region (time 10–25) remains relatively low (mostly 0.1–0.4) with smaller fluctuations than class 0.  
R3: Middle segment (time 25–40) rises from low to mid–high values (around 0.4–0.7) in one smoother upward movement.  
R4: Around time 40–55 there is a pronounced, wide valley where the signal falls from mid–high values down close to 0.1, then returns upward.  
R5: End segment (time 55–70) climbs back to mid values (around 0.4–0.6) with a gentle upward or flat trend, without additional extreme spikes.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts with a moderate–high value (around 0.5–0.7) rather than a steep drop from a very high value.  
R2: Does not contain a sustained long downward slope from high (≈0.7–0.8) to near the minimum (≈0.1–0.2); large drops, if present, are brief and followed quickly by recovery.  
R3: Mid-section (roughly the central third) shows several medium peaks and troughs, but the series tends to stay in a mid-range band (≈0.3–0.6) without a deep, long valley.  
R4: Any very low values (near 0.1–0.2) are short-lived spikes, not part of an extended flat low segment.  
R5: Overall, the shape appears more “wavy” and balanced around the mid-range than dominated by one long early fall and late rise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Begins at a high value (around 0.6–0.7 or above) and shows a clear, long downward slope in the first section, dropping towards low values (≈0.1–0.2).  
R2: After this initial fall, the signal climbs into a mid-range plateau (≈0.4–0.6) with relatively mild fluctuations.  
R3: In the later middle part, there is a prominent rise to a high peak (≈0.7–0.8) followed by a strong drop to near the minimum (≈0.0–0.1).  
R4: Near the end, the signal recovers from this deep low and rises steadily back to mid values (≈0.4–0.6).  
R5: The overall shape is characterized by “high → long fall → mid plateau → sharp high peak → very deep drop → late recovery,” with more pronounced long slopes than short, local wiggles.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series begins with a moderate value and quickly exhibits a sharp peak above 0.7 within the first 10 time steps.  
R2: After this early peak, the series drops to a low valley near or below 0.2 around time 10–15.  
R3: In the middle section (time 15–45), the series fluctuates around 0.4–0.6 with several small and medium peaks, without staying near zero for long.  
R4: One or more additional pronounced spikes above 0.6 occur in the second half (roughly between time 35–60), separated by clear drops.  
R5: The series ends in a mid-high region around 0.4–0.6, without a strong upward or downward trend in the last ~10 points.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a relatively high level (around 0.6–0.7) and shows a strong, mostly monotonic decrease down to near 0.1–0.2 within the first 10–15 time steps.  
R2: After this early deep valley, the series rises to a mid-level plateau (around 0.4–0.6) and remains there with only mild fluctuations through the middle part (time 15–40).  
R3: In the second half, the series contains one very sharp, tall peak above 0.7, immediately followed by a steep drop back to near 0.1–0.2.  
R4: Following this large peak–drop pattern, the series climbs again from the low value toward a mid-level region (around 0.4–0.6) in the last ~10–15 time steps.  
R5: Overall, the dominant large-scale pattern is “high → low → middle plateau → very high spike → very low → middle,” with clear long upward and downward trends rather than many irregular oscillations.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: One of the highest peaks (≥0.8) occurs in the first third of the series (roughly between indices 0 and 20).  
R2: After that early peak, the signal tends to drop to a low valley (≤0.2) before index ~20.  
R3: In the middle of the series (indices ~20–45), the values fluctuate moderately around 0.4–0.6 without very deep valleys.  
R4: A pronounced valley (≤0.2) appears in the last third of the series (indices ~45–60), immediately followed by a sharp rebound.  
R5: Throughout the series, multiple peaks and valleys are present, but no long, nearly flat segment dominates.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a moderate–high value (~0.6–0.7) and then decreases steeply to a clear low valley (≤0.2) within the first third (indices ~0–15).  
R2: After this early valley, the signal rises into a mid-level plateau in the central region (indices ~15–35) with values mostly between 0.4 and 0.6.  
R3: A tall peak (≥0.7) appears around the end of the central region (indices ~35–45).  
R4: Shortly after this peak, there is a pronounced drop to a deep valley (≤0.1–0.15) between indices ~45 and 55.  
R5: In the final part (indices ~55–70), the series climbs back to mid-level values (~0.4–0.6) and remains relatively smoother than in the first half.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Early segment (indices ~0–15) contains a sharp narrow positive spike reaching near the global maximum, followed immediately by a deep trough below 0.2.  
R2: After the early spike–trough pattern, the mid section (indices ~15–40) stays in a moderate band, mostly between 0.3 and 0.6, with only gentle undulations.  
R3: In the late-mid region (indices ~40–55) there is another pronounced dip below 0.2 followed by a rapid rise back above 0.6.  
R4: The final segment (indices ~55–70) stabilizes again in a moderate band roughly between 0.3 and 0.6, without extreme peaks or valleys.  
R5: Overall, large excursions (very high peaks or very low troughs) are brief and separated by longer, relatively flat, mid-level stretches.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Early segment (indices ~0–15) shows a broad, smooth decline from a high value (~0.7) down toward 0.2–0.1, without a very narrow spike.  
R2: After this smooth fall, the mid section (indices ~15–40) exhibits a gentle rise to a plateau around 0.4–0.6, with low-amplitude oscillations.  
R3: In the late-mid region (indices ~40–55) there is a long, steep drop from around 0.7–0.8 down close to 0 (≤0.1) over several consecutive points.  
R4: The recovery from this deep minimum back to mid-range values (0.4–0.6) is also relatively smooth and extended, not an immediate sharp rebound.  
R5: Overall, extreme low values persist over several consecutive points, and major transitions (up or down) are smoother and more prolonged than in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts with a moderate value (around 0.4–0.6) and shows one early sharp upward spike reaching at least about 0.8.  
R2: Has at least one pronounced deep dip below about 0.2 somewhere after this early spike.  
R3: The middle section (roughly one‑third to two‑thirds of the length) oscillates around a mid‑level (0.3–0.6) without staying low for long.  
R4: Contains at least two distinct peaks above about 0.6 separated by a lower valley.  
R5: Ends in the mid‑range (around 0.4–0.6) rather than very low or very high.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts relatively high (around 0.6–0.8) and then drops quickly into a clear low segment near 0.1–0.2.  
R2: After this first deep drop, rises to a plateau in the middle of the series that stays mostly between 0.4 and 0.6 with small fluctuations.  
R3: Contains one dominant tall peak in the later middle part, reaching around 0.7–0.8, followed by a steep fall.  
R4: After this dominant peak, exhibits a second low region close to 0.0–0.2 before recovering.  
R5: Ends by recovering from the second low to a moderate level (around 0.4–0.6) without another large spike.
